name: Mimicry Plague
description: 20% of all loot chests become Mimics when opened.
tradeoffDescription: Mimics count as minions — adventurers gain XP from killing them.

name: Taxation of Souls
description: Adventurers lose 5% max HP when entering a new room.
tradeoffDescription: Adventurers are already weakened on kill — you gain slightly less Soul Essence per death.

name: Gravitational Anomaly
description: Projectiles move 50% slower. Melee deals 20% more damage.
tradeoffDescription: Great against Rangers and Mages — but makes your melee minions more predictable and easier to dodge.

name: Cursed Fountains
description: Healing fountain rooms deal damage instead of healing.
tradeoffDescription: None. Adventurers who relied on your healing fountains will be unpleasantly surprised.

name: No Health Regeneration
description: Adventurers cannot heal while sleeping in the dungeon.
tradeoffDescription: None. Pure punishment for anyone trying to rest and recover inside your dungeon.

name: Memory Fog
description: Adventurers who sleep in the dungeon forget 50% of what they learned.
tradeoffDescription: They heal faster while sleeping — but wake up confused about where the traps are.

name: Eternal Night
description: Adventurers cannot see past one room in any direction.
tradeoffDescription: Your minions also have significantly reduced patrol range.

name: Bloodbound
description: Your minions deal +50% damage but die permanently instead of respawning overnight.
tradeoffDescription: Every minion lost is gone forever. Your dungeon slowly hollows out if you're not careful.

name: Hunger
description: Adventurers lose 1 HP every 30 seconds spent in the dungeon.
tradeoffDescription: Slow attrition rewards patient dungeon layouts over burst-damage rooms.

name: Knowledge Is Pain
description: Adventurers take +10% damage in rooms they have already cleared.
tradeoffDescription: First-timers are completely unaffected. Guilds start sending rookies to scout first instead of their veterans.

name: Paranoia Protocol
description: All chests, doors, and fountains show a visible '10% trap chance' indicator — whether or not they're actually trapped.
tradeoffDescription: Adventurers see '10%' on everything, including safe objects. The uncertainty is the trap.

name: Spectral Reinforcements
description: Ghosts of previously killed adventurers appear as hostile phantoms in the rooms where they died.
tradeoffDescription: Spectral adventurers deal spirit damage that also affects your living minions. High-kill rooms become dangerous for everyone.

name: Loot Curse
description: All loot dropped in the dungeon is cursed. Adventurers who pick it up gain the stat bonus but also a hidden worsening debuff.
tradeoffDescription: A Cleric in the party can cleanse the curse entirely, neutralizing this mechanic. Greedy adventurers hoard cursed loot faster.
